author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

On the words of the Gospel, John v. 25,’Verily, verily, I say unto you, the hour cometh, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the son of God; and they that hear shall live,’ etc.; and on the words of the apostle, ‘things which eye saw not,’ etc., 1 Cor. ii. 9.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John v. 25,’Verily, verily, I say unto you, the hour cometh, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the son of God; and they that hear shall live,’ etc.; and on the words of the apostle, ‘things which eye saw not,’ etc., 1 Cor. ii. 9.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our hope, Brethren, is not of this present time, nor of this world, nor in that happiness whereby men are blinded that forget God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This ought we above all things to know, and in a Christian heart hold fast, that we were not made Christians for the good things of the present time, but for something else which God at once promiseth, and man doth not yet comprehend.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For of this good it is said, “That eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither hath it entered into the heart of man, what things God hath prepared for them that love Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because then this good, so great, so excellent, so ineffable, fell not in with man’s understanding, it required God’s promise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what hath been promised him, man blind of heart doth not now comprehend; nor can it be shown to him at present, what he will one day be to whom the promise is given.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so an infant child, if he could understand the words of one speaking, when himself could neither speak, nor walk, nor do anything, but feeble as we see he is, unable to stand, requiring the assistance of others, were able only to understand him who should speak to him and tell him, “Lo, as thou seest me walking, working, speaking, after a few years thou shall be as I am;” as he considered himself and the other, though he would see what was promised; yet considering his own feebleness, would not believe, and yet he would see what was promised.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But with us infants, as it were, lying in this flesh and feebleness, that which is promised is at once great and is not seen; and so faith is aroused whereby we believe that we do not see that we may attain to see what we believe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whosoever derideth this faith, so as to think that he is not to believe in that he doth not see; when that shall come which he believed not, is put to shame: being confounded is separated, being separated, is condemned.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whoso shall have believed, is put aside at the right hand, and shall stand with great confidence and joy among those to whom it shall be said, “Come, blessed of My Father, receive the kingdom which hath been prepared for you from the beginning of the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Lord made an end when He spake these words, thus, “These shall go into everlasting burning, but the righteous into life eternal.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the life eternal which is promised us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because men love to live on this earth, life is promised them; and because they exceedingly fear to die, eternal life is promised them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What dost thou love?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This shalt thou have.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What dost thou fear?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou shalt not suffer it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This seemed to be enough for human infirmity, that it should be said, “Thou shalt have eternal life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This the mind of man can comprehend, by its present condition it can in some sort comprehend what is to be.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But by the imperfection of its present condition how far can it comprehend it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because he lives, and does not wish to die; he loves eternal life, he wishes to live always, never to die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they who shall be tormented in punishments, have even a wish to die, and cannot.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is no great thing then to live long, or to live for ever; but to live blessedly is a great thing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us love eternal life, and hereby may we know how greatly we ought to labour for eternal life, when we see men who love the present life, which lasts but for a time and must be brought to an end, labour so for it, that when the fear of death comes, they will do whatever they can, not to put away, but to put off death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How does a man labour, when death threatens, by flight, by concealment, by giving all he has, and redeeming himself, by toil, by endurance of torments and uneasinesses, by calling in physicians, and whatever else a man can do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, how that after exhausting all his labour and his means, he is but able to contrive to live a little longer; to live always, he is not able.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then men strive with so great labour, with so great efforts, so great a cost, such earnestness, such watchfulness, such carefulness, that they may live a little longer; how should they strive that they may live for ever?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if they are called wise, who by all means strive to put off death, and live a few days, that they lose not a few days: how foolish are they who so live as to lose the day eternal!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This then only can be promised us, that this gift of God may in whatever measure be sweet to us, from this which we have at present; seeing that it is of His gift we have it, that we live, that we are in health.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When then eternal life is promised, let us set before our eyes a life of such a kind, as to remove from it everything unpleasant which we suffer here.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is easier for us to find what is not there, than what is there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, here we live; we shall live there also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here we are in health when we are not sick, and there is no pain in the body; there we shall be in health also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when it is well with us in this life, we suffer no scourge; we shall suffer none there also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Suppose then a man here below living, in sound health, suffering no scourge; if any one were to grant him that he should be for ever so, and that this good estate should never cease, how greatly would he rejoice? how greatly be transported? how would he not contain himself in joy without pain, without torment, without end of life?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If God had promised us this only, which I have mentioned, which I have just now in such words as I was able, described and set forth; at what a price ought it to be purchased if it were to be sold, how great a sum ought to be given to buy it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Would all that thou hadst suffice, even though thou shouldest possess the whole world?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet it is to be sold; buy it if thou wilt.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And be not much disquieted for a thing so great, because of the largeness of the price.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Its price is no more than what thou hast.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now to procure any great and precious thing, thou wouldest get ready gold, or silver, or money, or any increase of cattle, or fruits, which might be produced in thy possessions, to buy this I know not what great and excellent thing, whereby to live in this earth happily.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Buy this too, if thou wilt.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not look for what thou hast, but for what thou art.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The price of this thing is thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Its price is what thou art thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give thine own self, and thou shalt have it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why art thou troubled? why disquieted?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Art thou going to seek for thine own self, or to buy thyself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, give thine own self as thou art, such as thou art to that thing, and thou shalt have it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But you will say, “I am wicked, and perhaps it will not accept me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By giving thyself to it, thou wilt be good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The giving thyself to this faith and promise, this is to be good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when thou shalt be good, thou wilt be the price of this thing; and shalt have, not only what I have mentioned, health, safety, life, and life without end; thou shalt not only have this, I will take away other things yet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There shall there be no weariness, and sleeping; there shall there be no hunger, and thirst; there shall there be no growing, and growing old; because there shall be no birth either where the numbers remain entire.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The number that is there is entire; nor is there any need for it to be increased, seeing there is no chance of diminution there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, how many things have I taken away, and I have not yet said what shall be there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, already there is life, and safety; no scourge, no hunger, no thirst, no failing, none of these; and yet I have not said, “what eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, nor hath ascended into the heart of man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if I have said it, it is false that is written, “Eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither hath it ascended into the heart of man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For whence should it ascend into my heart, that I should say “that which hath not ascended into the heart of man”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is believed, and not seen; not only not seen, but not even expressed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then is it believed, if it is not expressed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who believes what he doth not hear?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if he hear it that he may believe, it is expressed; if expressed, it is thought of; if thought of and expressed, then it entereth into the ears of men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And because it would not be expressed if it were not thought of, it hath ascended also into the heart of man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, already the mere proposing of so great a thing disturbs us, that we cannot put it forth clearly in words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who then can explain the thing itself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us attend to the Gospel; just now the Lord was speaking, and let us do what He said.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He that believeth in Me,” saith He, “passeth from death unto life, and cometh not into judgment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Verily I say unto you, that the hour shall come, and now is, when the dead shall hear the Voice of the Son of God, and they that hear shall live.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as the Father hath life in Himself, so hath He given to the Son to have life in Himself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By begetting Him He gave it; in that He begat, He gave it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Son is of the Father, not the Father of the Son; but the Father is the Father of the Son, and the Son is the Son of the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I say the Son is begotten of the Father, not the Father of the Son; and the Son was always, always therefore begotten.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who can comprehend this “always begotten”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when any man hears of one begotten, it occurs to him; “Therefore there was a time, when he who was begotten was not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What say we then?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not so; there was no time before the Son, for that “all things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If all things were made by Him, times also were made by Him; how could times be before the Son, by whom times were made?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take away then all times, the Son was with the Father always.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the Son were with the Father always, and yet the Son, He was begotten always; if begotten always, He who was begotten was always with Him That begat Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You will say, “This have I never seen, one begetting, and always with him whom he begat; but he that begat came first, and he that was begotten followed in time.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You say well, “I have never seen this;” for this appertains to “that which eye hath not seen.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do you ask how it may be expressed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It cannot be expressed; “For the ear hath not heard, neither hath it ascended unto the heart of man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be it believed and adored, when we believe, we adore; when we adore, we grow; when we grow, we comprehend.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as yet whilst we are in this flesh, as long as we are absent from the Lord, we are, with respect to the Holy Angels who see these things, infants to be suckled by faith, hereafter to be fed by sight.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so saith the Apostle, “As long as we are in the body we are absent from the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we walk by faith, not by sight.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We shall some day come to sight, which is thus promised us by John in his Epistle; “Dearly beloved, we are the sons of God, and it hath not yet appeared what we shall be.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We are the sons of God now by grace, by faith, by the Sacrament, by the Blood of Christ, by the redemption of the Saviour; “We are the sons of God, and it hath not yet appeared what we shall be.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We know that when He shall appear, we shall be like Him, for we shall see Him as He is.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, unto the comprehending of what are we being nourished up; lo, unto the embracing and the feeding on what are we being nourished up; yet so as that that which is fed on is not diminished, and he that feedeth is supported.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For now food supports us by eating it; but the food which is eaten, is diminished; but when we shall begin to feed on Righteousness, to feed on Wisdom, to feed on that Food Immortal, we are at once supported, and That Food is not diminished.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if the eye knows how to feed on light, and yet doth not diminish the light; for the light will be no less because it is seen by more; it feeds the eyes of more, and yet is as great as it was before: both they are fed, and it is not diminished; if God hath granted this to the light which He hath made for the eyes of the flesh, what is He Himself, the Light for the eyes of the heart?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then any choice food were praised to thee, on which thou wast to dine, thou wouldest prepare the stomach; God is praised to thee, prepare the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Behold what thy Lord saith to thee: “The hour shall come,” saith He, “and now is.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The hour shall come,” yea, that very hour, “now is, when”—what? “when the dead shall hear the Voice of the Son of God, and they that shall hear shall live.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They then that shall not hear, shall not live.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “They that shall hear”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They that shall obey.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “They that shall hear”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They that shall believe and obey, they shall live.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then before they believed and obeyed, they lay dead; they walked, and were dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What availed it to them, that they walked, being dead?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet if any among them were to die a bodily death, they would run, get ready the grave, wrap him up, carry him out, bury him, the dead, the dead; of whom it is said, “Let the dead bury their dead.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such dead as these are in such wise raised by the Word of God, as to live in faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who were dead in unbelief, are aroused by the Word.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of this hour said the Lord, “The hour shall come, and now is.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For with His Own Word did He raise them that were dead in unbelief; of whom the Apostle says, “Arise thou that sleepest, and rise up from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the resurrection of hearts, this is the resurrection of the inner man, this is the resurrection of the soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But this is not the only resurrection, there remains a resurrection of the body also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whoso riseth again in soul, riseth again in body to his blessedness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in soul all do not rise again; in body all are to rise again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In soul, I say, all do not rise again; but they that believe and, obey; for, “They that shall hear shall live.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But as the Apostle says, “All men have not faith.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then all men have not faith, all men do not rise again in soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When thy hour of the resurrection of the body shall come, all shall rise again; be they good or bad, all shall rise again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whoso first riseth again in soul, to his blessedness riseth again in body; whoso doth not first rise again in soul, riseth again in body to his curse.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whoso riseth again in soul, riseth again in body unto life; whoso riseth not again in soul, riseth again in body unto punishment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seeing then that the Lord hath impressed upon us this resurrection of souls, unto which we ought all to hasten, and to labour that we may live therein, and living persevere even unto the end, it remained for Him to impress upon us the resurrection of bodies also, which is to be at the end of the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now hear how He hath impressed this too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When He had said, “Verily I say unto you, The hour shall come, and now is, when the dead,” that is, the unbelievers, “shall hear the Voice of the Son of God,” that is, the Gospel, “and they that shall hear,” that is, that shall obey, “shall live,” that is, shall be justified, and shall be unbelievers no longer; when, I say, He had said this, forasmuch as He saw that we had need to be instructed as to the resurrection of the flesh also, and were not to be left thus, He went on and said, “For as the Father hath life in Himself, so hath He given to the Son to have life in Himself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This refers to the resurrection of souls, to the quickening of souls.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then He added, “And hath given Him power to execute judgment also, because He is the Son of Man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This Son of God, is Son of Man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if the Son of God had continued the Son of God, and had not been made the Son of Man, He would not have delivered the sons of men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who had made man, was Himself made that which He made, that what He made might not perish.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But He was in such wise made the Son of Man, as to continue the Son of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He was made Man by assuming that which He was not, not by losing That which He was; continuing God, He was made Man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He took thee, He was not consumed in thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As such then came He to us, the Son of God, and Son of Man, the Maker and the Made, the Creator and the Created; the Creator of His mother, Created of His mother; such came He to us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In respect of His being the Son of God, He saith, “The hour shall come, and now is, when the dead shall hear the Voice of the Son of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not say, “Of the Son of Man;” for He was impressing the truth, wherein He is equal to the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And they that shall hear shall live.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as the Father hath life in Himself, so hath He given to the Son to have life in Himself;” not by participation, but in our God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But He, the Father, hath life in Himself; and He begat such a Son as should have life in Himself; not be made a partaker of life, but Himself be Life, of which life we should be partakers; that is, should have life in Himself, and Himself be Life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But that He should be made the Son of Man, He took from us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Son of God in Himself; that He should be the Son of Man, He took from us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Son of God of That which is His Own, Son of Man of ours.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That which is the less, took He from us; That which is the more, gave He to us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thus He died in that He is the Son of Man, not in that He is the Son of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet the Son of God died; but He died in respect to the flesh, not in respect to “the Word which was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then in that He died, He died of that which was ours; in that we live, we live of That which is His.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He could not die of That which was His own, nor could we live of that which is our own.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As God then, as the Only-Begotten, as equal with Him who begat Him, did the Lord Jesus impress this upon us, that if we hear, we shall live.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But, saith He, “He hath given Him power to execute judgment also, because He is the Son of Man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then that Form is to come to judgment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Form of Man is to come to judgment; therefore He said, “He hath given Him power to execute judgment also, because He is the Son of Man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Judge here shall be the Son of Man; here shall That Form judge which was judged.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear and understand: the Prophet had said this already, “They shall look on Him whom they pierced.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That Very Form shall they see which they smote with a spear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He shall sit as Judge, Who stood at the judge’s seat.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He shall condemn the real criminals, Who was made a criminal falsely.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He shall come Himself, That Form shall come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This you find in the Gospel too; when before the eyes of His disciples He was going into heaven, they stood and looked on, and the Angelic voice spake, “Ye men of Galilee, why stand ye,” etc. “This Jesus shall come in like manner as ye see Him going into heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “shall come in like manner”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Shall come in this Very Form.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For “He hath given Him power to execute judgment, because He is the Son of Man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now see on what principle this was behoveful and right, that they who were to be judged might see the Judge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they who were to be judged were both good and bad.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It remained that in the Judgment the Form of the servant should be manifested both to good and bad, the Form of God be reserved for the good alone.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what is it that the good are to receive?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Behold I am now expressing that which I did not express a little above; and yet in expressing I do not express it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I said that there we shall be in sound health, shall be safe, shall be living, shall be without scourges, without hunger and thirst, without failing, without loss of our eyes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All this I said; but what we shall have more, I said not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We shall see God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now this will be so great, yea so great a thing will it be, that in comparison of it, all the rest is nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I said that we shall be living, that we shall be safe and sound, that we shall suffer no hunger and thirst, that we shall not fall into weariness, that sleep will not oppress us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All this, what is it to that happiness, whereby we shall see God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because then God cannot be now manifested as He is, whom nevertheless we shall see; therefore, “what eye hath not seen, nor ear heard,” this the good shall see, this shall the godly see, this the merciful shall see, this shall the faithful see, this shall they see who shall have a good lot in the resurrection of the body, for that they have had a good obedience in the resurrection of the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Shall then the wicked man see God too? of whom Isaiah saith, “Let the ungodly be taken away, that he see not the Glory of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Both the ungodly and the godly then shall see that Form; and when the sentence, “Let the ungodly be taken away that he see not the Glory of God,” shall have been pronounced; it remains that as to the godly and the good, that be fulfilled which the Lord Himself promised, when He was here in the flesh, and seen not by the good only, but by the evil also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He spake amongst the good and evil, and was seen of all, as God, hidden, as Man, manifested; as God ruling men, as Man appearing among men: He spake, I say, among them, and said, “Whoso loveth Me, keepeth My commandments; and he that loveth Me, shall be loved of My Father, and I will love him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And as if it were said to Him, And what wilt Thou give him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And “I will,” He saith, “manifest Myself to him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When did He say this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When He was seen by men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When did He say this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When He was seen even by them, by whom He was not loved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then was He to manifest Himself to them that loved Him, save in Such a Form, as they who loved Him then saw not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, seeing that the Form of God was being reserved, the Form of man manifested; by the Form of man, speaking to men, conspicuous and visible, He manifested Himself to all, both good and bad, He reserved Himself for them that loved Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When is He to manifest Himself to them that love Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: After the resurrection of the body, when “the ungodly shall be taken away that he see not the Glory of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For then “when He shall appear, we shall be like Him; for we shall see Him as He is.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is life eternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For all that we said before is nothing to that life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That we live, what is it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That we are in health, what is it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That we shall see God, is a great thing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is life eternal; this Himself hath said, “But this is life eternal, that they may know Thee the Only True God, and Jesus Christ whom Thou hast sent.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is life eternal, that they may know, see, comprehend, acquaint themselves with what they had believed, may perceive that which they were not yet able to comprehend.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then may the mind see what “eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither hath it ascended into the heart of man;” this shall be said to them at the end, “Come, ye blessed of My Father, receive the kingdom which hath been prepared for you from the beginning of the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Those wicked ones then shall go into everlasting burning.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the righteous, whither?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Into life eternal?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is life eternal?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “This is life eternal, that they may know Thee, the Only True God, and Jesus Christ, whom Thou hast sent.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Speaking then of the future resurrection of the body, and not leaving us thus, He saith, “He hath given Him power to execute judgment also, because He is the Son of Man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Marvel not at this, for the hour shall come.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not add in this place, “and now is;” because this hour shall be hereafter, because this hour shall be at the end of the world, because this shall be the last hour, shall be at the last trump.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Marvel not at this,” because I have said, “He hath given Him power to execute judgment also, because He is the Son of Man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Marvel not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this reason have I said this, because it behoves Him as Man to be judged by men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what men shall He judge?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Those whom He finds alive?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not only those, but what?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The hour shall come, when they that are in the graves.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How did He express those that are dead in the flesh?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “They who are in the graves,” whose corpses lie buried, whose ashes are covered up, whose bones are dispersed, whose flesh is flesh no more, and yet is entire to God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The hour shall come, when all that are in the graves shall hear His Voice, and shall come forth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be they good or bad, they shall hear the Voice, and shall come forth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All the bands of the grave shall be burst asunder; all that was lost, yea rather was thought to be lost shall be restored.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if God made man who was not, can He not re-fashion that which was?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I suppose when it is said, “God shall raise the dead again,” no incredible thing is said for it is of God, not of man, that it is said.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is a great thing which shall be done, yea, an incredible thing that shall be done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But let it not be incredible, for see, who It is That doeth it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He it is said shall raise thee, Who created thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wast not, and thou art; and once made, shalt thou not be?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God forbid thou shouldest think so!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God did something more marvellous when He made that which was not; and nevertheless He did make that which was not; and shall it be disbelieved that He is able to re-fashion that which was, by those very persons whom He made what they were not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is this the return we make to God, we who were not, and were made?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is this the return we make Him, that we will not believe that He is able to raise again what He hath made?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is this the return which His creature renders Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Have I therefore,” God saith to thee, “made thee, O man, before thou wast, that thou shouldest not believe Me, that thou shalt be what thou wast, who hast been able to be what thou wast not?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But you will say, “Lo, what I see in the tomb, is dust, ashes, bones; and shall this receive life again, skin, substance, flesh, and rise again? what? these ashes, these bones, which I see in the tomb?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: At least thou seest ashes, thou seest bones in the tomb; in thy mother’s womb there was nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This thou seest, ashes at least there are, and bones; before that thou wast, there was neither ashes, nor bones; and yet thou wast made, when thou wast not at all; and dost thou not believe that these bones (for in whatever state, of whatever kind they are, yet they are), shall receive the form again which they had, when thou hast received what thou hadst not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Believe; for if thou shalt believe this, then shall thy soul be raised up.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And thy soul shall be raised up “now;” “The hour shall come, and now is;” then to thy blessing shall thy flesh rise again, “when the hour shall come, that all that are in the graves shall hear His Voice, and shall come forth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou must not at once rejoice, because thou dost hear “and come forth;” hear what follows, “They that have done good unto the resurrection of life; but they that have done evil unto the resurrection of damnation.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Turning to the Lord, etc.
